article_title: Intel Launches Xeon 6+ 'Clearwater Forest' on 18A, Its First Data Center CPU on the New Node, With Up to 288 Cores

content_preview.title: Intel Launches Xeon 6+ 'Clearwater Forest' on 18A, Its First Data Center CPU on the New Node, With Up to 288 Cores
content_preview.summary: Intel's first 18A server chip ships with up to 288 Darkmont E-cores and 576 MB of L3 cache, claiming a 30% per-thread lead over AMD's 192-core EPYC 9965.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Intel launched its Xeon 6+ server processors, code-named Clearwater Forest, at Computex 2026, marking the first data center CPU built on the company's 18A manufacturing process, according to [The Register](https://www.theregister.com/systems/2026/06/01/intel-launches-288-core-clearwater-forest-xeon-6-on-18a-process/5248150). Intel describes Clearwater Forest as its first 18A-based server processor, as noted in an [Intel Newsroom](https://newsroom.intel.com/data-center/postcard-from-...

editor_notes.content_quality: Thorough, spec-dense hardware-launch News piece (774 words) on Clearwater Forest, careful to label Intel's vendor comparisons and to keep the two performance baselines distinct.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200). source-0 (The Register) confirms — verbatim — the up-to-288 Darkmont E-cores, the Xeon 6990E+ / 576 MB and 6960E+ / 432 MB / 144-core, the tile architecture ('twelve 24-core tiles fabbed on 18A … stacked atop three tiles built on Intel 3, which contain the memory controllers and L3 cache … a pair of I/O dies borrowed from … Xeon 6900P'), the 3.2 GHz turbo, 330–450 W TDP, 12-channel DDR5-8000, the 17% IPC gain, and the drop-into-existing-boards compatibility. source-1 (TechTimes) confirms the 30%-per-thread-vs-EPYC-9965 (192-core) claim, the 55%-perf/watt-vs-6780E claim, the Dell/HPE/Lenovo/Supermicro availability, and the Tim Wilson 'bottlenecked by the CPU' quote. source-3 (Tom's Hardware) confirms the Kira Boyko 'most performant Xeon … scale-out workloads' quote, the Wilson SMT 'one or two physical cores are always better than two virtual cores' quote, and the wafer-allocation quotes. source-2 (Intel Newsroom) confirms the first-18A-server framing, the 17% IPC, and the Kevork Kechichian quote.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every fabrication-risk specific (288 cores, 576/432 MB L3, the twelve/three/two tile split, 3.2 GHz, 330–450 W, DDR5-8000, 17%, the 6990E+/6960E+/6780E/EPYC 9965 part numbers) is verbatim in a cited source, and every quote is correctly attributed. The article keeps the 30%-vs-AMD and 55%-vs-prior-gen baselines explicitly distinct (flagged in 'What We Don't Know'). The contributing agent also caught and fixed a broken inline link during its audit.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; vendor comparisons are clearly labeled as Intel-supplied and un-benchmarked.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — Clearwater Forest had only passing mentions in the archive; distinct from the recently-covered Samsung HBM5 and Lenovo Auto Twist hardware stories.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully-verified hardware-launch submission with model spec discipline. APPROVE.
